page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8369HB processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations show solid compute and memory throughput. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1579.99 and a multi-core score of 33336.85. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 1865.31 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 115.09 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, alongside a write speed of 52.5 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the server processed 31.19 million Redis SET operations per second and delivered a static web serving throughput of 23.9 GB/sec. For language model workloads, prompt processing reached 1656.63 tokens per second on a small 135M model and 47.54 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed provide reliable, non-burstable compute performance, making it highly suitable for CPU-bound tasks. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the complete absence of local storage represent clear tradeoffs, requiring external storage solutions and limiting its viability for memory-intensive databases. The instance is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate-scale CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8369HB processor. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong compute capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 33336.85 and a Passmark CPU score of 31763.08. It also demonstrates high throughput in data processing, achieving 31.19 million Redis SET operations per second and 23.9 GB/sec in static web serving. Given its high clock speed and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads, though the lack of local storage and low memory-to-core ratio are key tradeoffs. It is ideal for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads without local storage.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core compute-optimized server featuring high clock speeds and balanced memory throughput for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB processor with 32 physical cores at 3.3 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 33336.85
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-bound data processing
